metadata.description: Source registry for all data used in Luganda AI Studio datasets

sources.title: English to Luganda Easy Reading Handbook
sources.notes: Primary source. Contains structured vocabulary by category, grammar rules, verb tenses, and real dialogue examples. Very high quality for our purposes.
sources.license: Unknown — data extracted for educational/research use

sources.title: Lajenta Mulimu Amazzi
sources.notes: Good topical vocabulary coverage. Used for transport, road signs, and supplemental vocabulary. Some entries may have OCR issues from scanning.
sources.license: Unknown — data extracted for educational/research use

sources.title: Kiganda Proverbs: A Linguistic and Cultural Analysis
sources.notes: Contains approximately 80 Kiganda proverbs with analysis. 60 have been extracted with translations and themes. Remaining 20 need further extraction. Academic source with good contextual explanations.
sources.license: Academic — extracted for research/educational use only

vocabulary.notes: Missing some cooking vocabulary and local dishes
Missing some extended family terms and Luganda kinship system specifics

sentences.notes: Good foundation. Needs more conversational pairs, more beginner sentences, and more topic variety.
Good greetings coverage. Needs more formal/informal variants and multi-turn dialogue examples.

grammar.notes: Core vowel rules captured. Missing: tonal rules, vowel harmony patterns.
Core consonant rules captured. Missing: full consonant cluster documentation.
All 8 word classes documented. Missing: noun class agreement system (critical gap), adjective agreement tables.
4 main tenses documented with examples. Missing: negative forms, full subject prefix tables, aspect markers.

proverbs.notes: 60 of approximately 80 proverbs extracted. 20 more remain in dissertation. Themes covered: hardwork, wisdom, respect.

critical.why_it_matters: Chat assistant needs back-and-forth dialogue patterns to respond naturally
Users need to say 'I do not...' and 'He is not...' — currently undocumented
Adjectives, verbs, and pronouns change based on noun class — essential for correct grammar

important.why_it_matters: Health is a core use case — hospital phrases, symptoms, body systems
Knowing 'five' is not useful without 'give me five tomatoes'
Needed to score translation quality — cannot improve without measuring

future.why_it_matters: Voice input/output phase will need aligned audio+text pairs
Feedback loop for improving translation quality over time

total_summary.overall_completeness: low — strong foundation but far below minimum for reliable AI
